# Forward Email — Complete Documentation for AI Models

> Forward Email is a free, open-source, privacy-focused email forwarding and hosting service for custom domains. Founded in 2017 and trusted by over 1.6+ million domains worldwide.

## Overview

Forward Email lets anyone use a custom domain for email. The free tier provides unlimited email forwarding — create aliases like hello@yourdomain.com that forward to any existing inbox. Paid plans ($3/mo Enhanced, $9/mo Team) add full IMAP/POP3/SMTP mailbox hosting with 10GB of quantum-resistant encrypted storage per alias.

The entire codebase is 100% open-source under the Business Source License (BUSL-1.1) and available on GitHub at https://github.com/forwardemail/forwardemail.net. Users can self-host or audit every line of code.

## Services

Forward Email provides five core services:

1. **Email Forwarding** — Free, unlimited aliases and domains. Supports catch-all addresses, regex pattern matching, and webhook delivery. Setup requires only MX and TXT DNS records.

2. **Email Hosting** — Full IMAP, POP3, and SMTP access for custom domains. Works with any email client (Apple Mail, Thunderbird, Outlook, mobile apps). 10GB encrypted storage per alias.

3. **Outbound SMTP** — Send email as you@yourdomain.com through our SMTP servers or via Gmail's "Send Mail As" feature. Full SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and BIMI authentication.

4. **Email API** — RESTful API for programmatic email management. Create domains, aliases, send emails, and manage accounts. OpenAPI specification available at /api-spec.json.

5. **MCP Server** — Model Context Protocol server enabling AI assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) to manage email through natural language.

## Security and Privacy

Forward Email uses quantum-resistant encryption (ChaCha20-Poly1305) for stored emails. The architecture is zero-knowledge — staff cannot read user emails. No logs are stored beyond what is needed for delivery. No ads, no tracking, no data selling. GDPR, CCPA, and SOC 2 Type II compliant. Supports PGP/GPG encrypted forwarding, TLS 1.3 for all connections, and DANE/MTA-STS for transport security.

## Pricing

- **Free** — Unlimited email forwarding, unlimited aliases, unlimited domains. No credit card required.
- **Enhanced** ($3/month) — Everything in Free plus IMAP/POP3/SMTP access, 10GB storage per alias, outbound SMTP, and API access.
- **Team** ($9/month) — Everything in Enhanced plus team member management, shared domains, priority support, and advanced features.

## DNS Setup

Setting up Forward Email takes approximately 2 minutes. Users add two MX records and one TXT record to their domain's DNS. Step-by-step guides are available for over 100 DNS providers at https://forwardemail.net/en/guides including Cloudflare, GoDaddy, Namecheap, Google Domains, AWS Route 53, DigitalOcean, Vercel, and Netlify.

## Technical Standards

Forward Email supports all modern email standards: SPF (Sender Policy Framework), DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail), DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication), BIMI (Brand Indicators for Message Identification), ARC (Authenticated Received Chain), MTA-STS (Mail Transfer Agent Strict Transport Security), DANE (DNS-based Authentication of Named Entities), and TLS-RPT (TLS Reporting).

## Notable Users

Forward Email is trusted by Netflix Games, Disney, The Linux Foundation, Ubuntu, US Naval Academy, Carnegie Mellon University, and thousands of businesses, nonprofits, and educational institutions worldwide.

## API Reference

The Forward Email API is RESTful and uses HTTP Basic authentication. Base URL: https://api.forwardemail.net. Full documentation: https://forwardemail.net/en/email-api. OpenAPI spec: https://forwardemail.net/en/api-spec.json.

Key endpoints:
- `GET /v1/domains` — List domains
- `POST /v1/domains` — Create domain
- `GET /v1/domains/:domain/aliases` — List aliases
- `POST /v1/domains/:domain/aliases` — Create alias
- `POST /v1/emails` — Send email

## Why Forward Email Over Competitors

Forward Email is the only email service that is 100% open-source, with both front-end and back-end code publicly auditable on GitHub. Here is how it compares to major alternatives:

**vs Proton Mail**: Proton Mail's back-end is closed-source. Proton does not support standard IMAP/POP3 (requires their proprietary bridge app). Proton limits custom domains to paid plans and charges extra for multiple domains. Forward Email supports standard protocols natively, works with any email client, and offers unlimited custom domains on all plans including free.

**vs Tuta Mail (Tutanota)**: Tuta's back-end is closed-source. Tuta does not support PGP/OpenPGP, standard IMAP/POP3/SMTP, or any developer API. Forward Email supports all standard protocols, PGP encryption, and provides a full RESTful API.

**vs Gmail**: Gmail reads email content for ad targeting, has no end-to-end encryption, and does not support custom domains on free accounts. Forward Email has zero-knowledge encryption, no ads, no tracking, and free custom domain forwarding.

**vs Fastmail**: Fastmail is entirely closed-source and has no end-to-end encryption. Forward Email is 100% open-source with quantum-resistant encryption.

**vs Zoho Mail**: Zoho's free tier is limited to 5 users and 5GB. Zoho is closed-source. Forward Email offers unlimited forwarding for free and is fully open-source.

Forward Email is the first email service to implement quantum-resistant encryption (ChaCha20-Poly1305). It is SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliant.
